subUnits.title: 9.1 Basis of Growth – Cell Division, Cell Enlargement and Cell Differentiation
notes: Growth is a permanent, irreversible increase in size, mass, volume, or number of cells in an organism.
• Involves increase in size, mass, or number of cells
• Accompanied by cell division, cell enlargement, and cell differentiation
Growth occurs through three main processes:
• Definition: Process by which a parent cell divides into two genetically identical daughter cells
c) Replaces worn-out or damaged cells
d) Basis for asexual reproduction in some organisms
• Nigerian context: Mitosis in cassava meristems enables vegetative propagation and growth
• Definition: Increase in cell size after division
a) Cells absorb water by osmosis
b) Vacuoles expand, pushing cytoplasm against cell wall
c) Cell wall stretches and new material is deposited
• Important in plant growth (especially in stems and roots)
• Nigerian example: Enlargement of cells in yam tubers during storage organ development
• Definition: Process by which unspecialized cells become specialized in structure and function
a) Cells develop specific structures for specific functions
b) Controlled by gene expression and environmental signals
c) Results in formation of tissues and organs
- Plant: Root hair cells for absorption, guard cells for gas exchange
- Animal: Red blood cells for oxygen transport, nerve cells for signaling
• Nigerian context: Differentiation in cassava roots leads to starch storage cells
Hormones are chemical messengers produced in small quantities that regulate growth and development in organisms.
• Nigerian context: Growth hormone deficiencies affect child development in Nigeria
- Essential for normal growth and development
- Important for brain development in children
• Deficiency: Cretinism (in children), Goiter, reduced metabolism
• Nigerian context: Iodine deficiency in some Nigerian regions causes goiter
- Essential for energy metabolism and growth
• Nigerian context: Rising diabetes cases in Nigeria affect growth and health
- Control development of secondary sexual characteristics
- Influence bone growth and closure of growth plates
• Nigerian context: Puberty timing varies among Nigerian adolescents due to nutrition and genetics
• Nigerian example: Auxins regulate growth direction in maize and cassava
• Nigerian example: Gibberellins used to improve yam and cassava yields
- Work with auxins to control growth
• Nigerian example: Cytokinins used in tissue culture for crop propagation
Growth can be measured using various parameters:
• Nigerian context: Height measurement used in school health programs
• Nigerian context: Weight monitoring in infant welfare clinics
• Definition: Mass of organism after all water has been removed
• Measured by: Drying sample in oven, then weighing
• Advantage: Most accurate measure of growth (not affected by water content)
• Nigerian context: Used in agricultural research to measure crop growth
• Nigerian example: Measuring yam tuber size for yield assessment
• Nigerian context: Used in microbiology labs for bacterial growth studies
- Stationary phase: Growth slows and stabilizes
- Decline phase: Growth stops or reverses
• Nigerian example: Growth curve of maize plants from planting to harvest
• Nigerian context: Genetic variation affects growth rates in Nigerian crops and livestock
• Nigerian context: Malnutrition affects child growth in some Nigerian communities
• Nigerian context: Hormonal treatments used in livestock farming
• Temperature, light, water, space affect growth
• Nigerian context: Climate affects crop growth patterns across Nigeria
• Nigerian context: Malaria and other diseases affect child growth in Nigeria
• Teacher demonstrates growth by students measuring their height (length) and weight
• Students record measurements and plot growth curves
• Discussion: What factors affect human growth?
• Grow bean or maize seeds in pots
• Nigerian context: Relate to crop growth monitoring in Nigerian farms
• Observe under microscope for mitotic stages
• 'Growth is only increase in size': Growth includes cell division, enlargement, and differentiation
• 'All growth is visible': Some growth occurs at cellular level and is not immediately visible
• 'Hormones only affect animals': Plants also use hormones to regulate growth
• 'Growth continues throughout life': Most organisms have definite growth periods
• Understanding growth helps in agriculture (crop improvement, livestock management)
• Knowledge of growth regulation important for public health (child development, nutrition)
• Growth measurement techniques used in Nigerian schools, clinics, and research
• Hormonal knowledge applied in Nigerian agriculture and medicine
• Growth is a fundamental characteristic of living organisms
• It occurs through cell division, enlargement, and differentiation
• Hormones play crucial roles in regulating growth in plants and animals
• Growth can be measured using various quantitative methods
• In Nigeria, understanding growth supports agriculture, health, and scientific advancement

subUnits.youtubeQuery: Growth cell division hormones biology SSS1 site:youtube.com

opts: Permanent, irreversible increase in size, mass, or cell number
quiz.explanation: Growth involves permanent increases through cell division, enlargement, and differentiation, and is irreversible.

quiz.q: The process by which unspecialized cells become specialized is called:
quiz.explanation: Cell differentiation is the process where cells develop specific structures and functions for specific roles.

quiz.q: The hormone produced by the pituitary gland that stimulates growth is:
quiz.explanation: Growth hormone (somatotropin) from the pituitary gland stimulates cell division, enlargement, and protein synthesis.

quiz.q: A deficiency of thyroxine in children can cause:
quiz.explanation: Thyroxine deficiency in children causes cretinism, characterized by stunted growth and mental retardation.

quiz.q: The most accurate method of measuring growth is:
quiz.explanation: Dry mass is the most accurate because it is not affected by water content, though it is a destructive method.

quiz.q: A plant hormone that promotes stem elongation is:
quiz.explanation: Gibberellins stimulate stem elongation, seed germination, and flowering in plants.

quiz.q: In Nigerian agriculture, understanding growth helps farmers to:
opts: Monitor crop development and improve yields
quiz.explanation: Knowledge of growth processes helps farmers optimize conditions for crop development and maximize yields.

quiz.q: The phase of the sigmoid growth curve where growth is most rapid is the:
quiz.explanation: The log or exponential phase is characterized by rapid, exponential growth as cells divide rapidly.

quiz.q: A factor that can stunt growth in Nigerian children is:
quiz.explanation: Diseases like malaria can impair nutrient absorption and metabolism, leading to stunted growth in children.

quiz.q: Understanding growth regulation is important for Nigerian public health because it:
opts: Informs child development monitoring and nutrition programs
quiz.explanation: Knowledge of growth regulation supports child health monitoring, nutrition interventions, and disease prevention in Nigeria.
